content:
First, because 
 LHS = f(x−1)+f(x)+f(x+1)  
has the same degree as f, while  
 RHS = f(x)²/(2013 x)  
has degree 2·deg f −1,  
equating degrees forces deg f = 1.  Write  
 f(x)=Ax+B, A≠0.  
Then  
 LHS = A(x−1)+B + A x+B + A(x+1)+B = 3A x+3B = 3(Ax+B),  
and  
 RHS = (Ax+B)²/(2013 x).  
The identity  
 3(Ax+B) = (Ax+B)²/(2013 x)  
for all x≠0  
⇔ (Ax+B)[Ax+B − 6039 x] = 0 as a polynomial.  
Since f is not identically zero, we must have  
 Ax+B −6039 x ≡ 0 ⇒ A=6039, B=0.  
Hence the unique nonconstant solution is f(x)=6039 x, so f(1)=6039.  

Answer: \boxed{6039}.
